items.title: Dietary footprints across populations
items.abstract: Diets affect both human and planetary health. Recent research reveals how dietary footprints vary across demographic groups and offers new data and methods for future studies.

items.title: Cropland deterioration widens yield gaps
items.abstract: The degradation of agricultural land poses a vital threat to productivity and is, in some cases, hard to reverse. Curbing this trend is essential to avoid cascading impacts on natural ecosystems, biodiversity and human health.
items.authors: Christian Folberth

items.title: Dietary environmental impacts and quality differ by socio-demographic characteristics
items.abstract: Dietary intake and supply chain data from 165 countries reveal demographic disparities in diet-related environmental impacts. Impacts are greatest among young, highly educated urban men, and healthier diets are associated with high plant-based food intake and lower environmental footprints.
items.authors: Oliver Taherzadeh, Arthur Schoorlemmer, Paul Behrens et al.

items.title: Land degradation is associated with larger crop yield gaps across global croplands
items.abstract: Land degradation is widely recognized as a threat to food security. Using high-resolution global data on multiple land degradation indicators and crop yield gaps, the study shows that greater historical land degradation is associated with larger yield gaps in major food-producing regions. These findings highlight the need for sustainable agricultural intensification and agri-environmental policies to reduce land degradation and protect future food production.
items.authors: Hadi Hadi, D. K. Ray, P. Borrelli et al.

items.title: Mechanistic effects of β-glucan and cellulose on the microstructure, rheology and digestion behaviour of milk fat-in-water emulsions stabilised by whey protein or rapeseed protein
items.authors: Miroslaw M. Kasprzak, Kamila Goderska, Joanna Kruk et al.

items.title: A combined metabolomics and flavoromics approach reveals dynamic changes in non-volatile and volatile compounds of Tieguanyin tea during roasting process
items.authors: Feifei Shi, Peng Liang, Shuyu Guo et al.

items.title: Flavor profile and deterioration mechanism of Niubie juice during low-temperature storage based on flavoromics and metabolomics
items.authors: Su Xu, Hailin Xu, Linyao Song et al.

items.authors: Katalin Lányi, Viktor Ágai, Anna Buzás et al.

items.title: The exclusion paradox: why food system reform requires conditional engagement with industry in policy deliberation for healthy and sustainable diets
items.authors: Jimmy Chun Yu Louie

items.authors: Foteini F. Parlapani, Stephen O'Rourke, Ioannis S. Boziaris

items.title: From sensory barriers to market acceptance: A multi-group generalized structural equation modelling of willingness to buy for Spirulina-enriched crackers across food-related lifestyle segments
items.authors: A.S.H. Wahyuningtyas, Gladys Oryz Berlian, Riyanti Isaskar et al.
